title: Robinhood Markets 'zombie' stock is at risk of falling to zero, New Constructs says
text: Robinhood Markets Inc. HOOD, was the latest target of New Constructs LLC, the independent research firm that has taken aim at meme stocks AMC Entertainment Holdings Inc. AMC, +3.27% and GameStop Corp. GME, +1.97% by calling them "zombie" companies facing severe cash burn. In a note titled "Zombie Stock: Robinhood -- Democratizing Foolish Investing," New Constructs Chief Executive David Trainer said Robinhood's commission-free trading platform made the "reckless" meme stock trade possible.

title: U.S. SEC probing Robinhood compliance with short selling rules -filing
text: Retail trading platform Robinhood Markets Inc disclosed in a filing on Wednesday that the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has been probing the company's compliance with short selling rules since October 2021.

title: Robinhood Stock Jumps After Job Cuts
text: Robinhood Markets Inc (NASDAQ:HOOD) is up 11.8% at $10.31, after the trading platform reported second-quarter losses of 34 cents per share -- slightly less than the 37-cent loss anticipated by analysts -- and a 44% decline in revenue due to a dip in trading activity.

title: Robinhood lays off an additional 780 employees as the crypto contagion spreads
text: (Kitco News) - The fallout from the crypto market collapse in the second quarter continues to spread as the popular online brokerage firm Robinhood announced on Tuesday that it would be laying off approximately 23% of its staff “as part of a broader company reorganization into a General Manager (GM) structure.”

title: Trading slump hits Robinhood's revenues as retail investors tap out
text: In what came as little surprise to industry onlookers in Wednesday, NASDAQ-listed trading platform Robinhood posted underwhelming second-quarter results underscored by a sharp decline in retail investment customers. Underlying earnings (EBITDA) were negative US$80mln, with net losses of US$295mln equating to -US$0.34 per diluted share.

title: Robinhood cuts 23% of its workforce as fewer users trade
text: Robinhood Markets said Tuesday it's cutting nearly a quarter of its workforce, as crashing cryptocurrency prices and a turbulent stock market keep more customers off its trading app.

text: Just months after cutting 9% of its workforce, Robinhood on Tuesday announced plans to lay off another 23%.

title: Robinhood posts 44% slump in revenue, slashes headcount by 23%
text: After reporting slumping revenues, Robinhood announced they are cutting nearly a quarter of their its' full-time staff and doing internal restructuring.

title: Robinhood Cuts 23% of Staff as Brokerage Sees 34% Drop in Monthly Active Users
text: Robinhood, the popular brokerage app, will lay off about 23% of its staff as customers slow down trading, The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) wrote Tuesday (Aug. 2). This is the second time the company has made a round of layoffs this year, following a cut of 9% of its employees in April.

title: Robinhood posts 6% sequential increase in 2nd qtr revenue
text: Robinhood Markets Inc posted a 6% sequential increase in second quarter revenue despite a market sell-off in equities and cryptocurrencies.

text: Forbes is tracking the biggest layoffs as companies react to fears of a looming recession.

title: Robinhood cuts 23% of staff; CEO Vlad Tenev says, ‘This is on me'
text: It's been a volatile year for retail investment behemoth Robinhood. The fintech company is laying off 23% of its workforce, as first reported by the Wall Street Journal and confirmed by TechCrunch.

title: Robinhood Crypto Arm Slapped With $30M Fine by Regulators
text: Crypto companies are continuing to take note of the changing regulatory landscape around them, and Robinhood (NASDAQ: HOOD ) is no exception. In the last several years, the company has picked up significant scrutiny from regulators over its stock trading platform.

title: Robinhood's Crypto Arm to Pay $30M for Anti-Money Laundering, Consumer Protection Violations
text: The fine is the latest in a series of lawsuits and alleged violations against the investing app.

title: Robinhood Crypto Unit Faces $30M Fine for AML, Cybersecurity Violations
text: The New York State Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) has fined the cryptocurrency trading unit of online brokerage Robinhood $30 million for alleged violations of anti-money laundering (AML) and cybersecurity regulations, The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported Tuesday (Aug. 2).

title: Robinhood Crypto fined $30 million by NYDFS
text: The New York State Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) has imposed a $30 million fine on Robinhood Crypto, the digital assets trading arm of Robinhood Markets Inc. (NASDAQ: HOOD), the regulator said in an announcement on Tuesday.

title: Robinhood's Crypto Arm Hit With $30 Million Fine By State Regulator
text: The fine comes after Robinhood allegedly violated cybersecurity and anti-money laundering regulations.

title: Robinhood's Crypto Arm Fined $30 Million Over Anti-Money Laundering Failures
text: New York's top financial regulator fined the crypto arm of broker Robinhood for allegedly breaching anti-money laundering and cybersecurity rules.

title: Robinhood's crypto division fined $30 million by New York financial regulator
text: Robinhood's crypto division was fined $30 million for "significant failures" in areas of anti-money laundering and cybersecurity regulations.
